race.name: Skarverennet
race.tagline: Twelve thousand Norwegians skating across the Hardangervidda in April — Norway's biggest ski race on Europe's largest mountain plateau.

vitals.registration: Opens in autumn; sells out rapidly
vitals.aid_stations: Multiple feed stations across the mountain plateau

climate.description: Late April on the Hardangervidda means spring skiing with longer days but still winter conditions at altitude. Temperatures typically -5°C to +5°C. The open plateau is notoriously exposed to wind — severe weather can force cancellation or course changes.
challenges: Extreme wind exposure on the open Hardangervidda plateau
April weather can shift rapidly between winter and spring conditions
Sun-softened snow in afternoon warmth affects wax and glide
The course has been cancelled or shortened due to extreme wind

course.primary: Point-to-point across the Hardangervidda mountain plateau, the largest high-altitude plateau in Northern Europe. The course is fully exposed above treeline with dramatic mountain scenery. Undulating terrain with no shelter from wind.
course.surface: Machine-groomed tracks on open mountain plateau
course.grooming: High-quality grooming across open mountain terrain
features: Entirely above treeline on Europe's largest mountain plateau
Norway's largest ski race by participant count
Late April date means long daylight and spring skiing atmosphere
Dramatic exposed mountain landscape — no forest shelter
Near Finse — the highest station on the Bergen Railway (1,222m)

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Massive domestic participation and dramatic mountain scenery are the highlights. Late April date guarantees snow at altitude. Limited international draw and moderate competitive depth keep it below TIER 1 threshold.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes from base TIER 2 — stays TIER 2 (cannot enter TIER 1)

history.summary: Founded in 1974, Skarverennet has grown to become Norway's largest ski race by participant count, with capacity for over 12,000 skiers. The race crosses the Hardangervidda, Europe's largest mountain plateau, on a course that is entirely above treeline. The late April date takes advantage of reliable spring snow at altitude and provides a dramatic end to the Norwegian ski season. The name references the mountain Skarven near the course.
notable_facts: Norway's largest ski race by participant count — capacity over 12,000
Crosses the Hardangervidda, Northern Europe's largest mountain plateau
Late April date makes it one of the last major ski races of the season
Entirely above treeline — one of the most exposed courses in world skiing
Near Finse, where Roald Amundsen trained for his South Pole expedition
The Bergen Railway provides access — Finse is Norway's highest railway station

youtube_data.search_query: Skarverennet cross country ski race Geilo/Finse to Ustaoset area

videos.title: Driving thru Ustaoset, Hol, Norway
videos.description:
Ustaoset, Norway

Ustaoset is a village in Hol municipality, Viken County, Norway. It is located on Norwegian National Road 7. Ustaoset is situated 990 m above sea level and is the finish for the cross country ski event Skarverennet. Ustaoset first came to prominence in 1909 with the opening of the Bergen Line.

Ustaoset station on the Bergen line is located here.  On site is also a cottage town with about 900 cottages, hotels and mountain lodges.

 The name comes from the fact that the place is the estuary where the river Usteåne flows out of Ustevatn.

 Ustaoset is the finish line in the Skarverennet touring race.

🇳🇴norsk

Ustaoset ligger i Hol kommune i Viken. Ustaoset stasjon på Bergensbanen ligger her. På stedet ligger også en hytteby med cirka 900 hytter, hotell og fjellstuer.

Navnet kommer av at stedet er oset der elven Usteåne renner ut fra Ustevatn.

Ustaoset er målgang i turrennet Skarverennet.
tags: Off the beaten path travel in norway

videos.title: Skarverennet 2025: taking on a Norwegian cross country skiing marathon!
videos.description:
A bit different to our normal travel videos! I travel to Norway with some of our family to take on Skarverennet - a 39km cross country skiing race (so very nearly a marathon!).


Hi - we are Callum and Jan - and having decided to give up work in our 50's we are travelling through SE Asia over the next few months. We have already done a driving trip round Europe (Spain, France, Monaco, Italy, Croatia, more of Italy, Austria, Germany and Holland) in 2021, 10 weeks in Thailand in  Spring 2022 and 10 weeks in Peru and Colombia in Autumn 2022. We did do BLOGs for these (https://rossiesgoroaming.travel.blog/...) but decided to have a go at VLOGs as we have found many so useful in planning our current trip.

Our favourite VLOGs:
Paddy Doyle
   / @paddydoyle  .

Normally this would be about 2 m of snow at this time of year, but we're walking along the road. Normally all you can see here is snow, no bare rocks or heather, but as you can see, not the case this year.
Try to find the best route, avoiding the bare patches, the heather, the little streams and ponds that are scattering the tracks. To get uphill on cross country skis, you have to herringbone.
